@graph.name: Window Replacement - Capitol Improvements
@graph.description: Provia Door & Window Certified contractor replacing windows in Maryland, DC, and Virginia. ENERGY STAR certified, Low-E glass, argon gas filled windows including double-hung, casement, bay, bow, and slider styles.

itemOffered.name: Double-Hung Window Replacement
itemOffered.description: Both sashes tilt in for easy cleaning. Most popular window style. Available with Low-E glass and argon gas fill.

itemOffered.name: Casement Window Replacement
itemOffered.description: Crank-operated windows that open outward for maximum ventilation and an excellent air-tight seal when closed.

itemOffered.name: Bay & Bow Window Installation
itemOffered.description: Projecting multi-panel windows that add interior space and create a focal point.

itemOffered.name: Slider Window Replacement
itemOffered.description: Horizontal sliding windows ideal for wide, low openings. Low-maintenance with Low-E glass options.

itemOffered.name: Picture Window Replacement
itemOffered.description: Fixed-pane windows for maximum light and unobstructed views. Available with triple-pane glass.

itemOffered.name: Awning Window Replacement
itemOffered.description: Top-hinged windows that open outward — ideal for ventilation even during rain.

itemOffered.name: Energy-Efficient Window Replacement
itemOffered.description: ENERGY STAR certified Provia windows with Low-E coatings, argon gas fill, and warm-edge spacer technology.

mainEntity.name: What makes a window energy efficient?

acceptedAnswer.text: Energy-efficient windows combine Low-E glass coatings, argon or krypton gas between panes, warm-edge spacer systems, and insulated frames. We install Provia windows that meet or exceed ENERGY STAR requirements for the Mid-Atlantic climate zone.

mainEntity.name: What window brands do you install?

acceptedAnswer.text: We are a Provia Door & Window Certified contractor. Provia windows are built in the USA and known for quality vinyl construction, ENERGY STAR performance, and excellent warranties.

mainEntity.name: How many windows can you replace in a day?

acceptedAnswer.text: Our crews typically replace 8–15 windows per day. A full-home window replacement is usually completed in 1–2 days.

Low-E glass has a microscopically thin metallic coating that reflects infrared energy. It keeps heat inside during winter and reflects solar heat outside during summer, significantly reducing energy bills.
